findings.title: Embedded default database credentials in generated Compose template
findings.description: The SKILL.md includes a Python template generator that emits Docker Compose configurations containing hardcoded database connection strings and default PostgreSQL credentials (`postgres:postgres`). While these appear to be local development defaults rather than exfiltration logic, embedding credentials in generated configuration can expose sensitive patterns, encourage insecure deployments, and lead to accidental reuse outside development.
findings.remediation: Replace hardcoded credentials with environment-variable placeholders (for example `${DATABASE_URL}` and `${POSTGRES_PASSWORD}`), document that users should supply secrets via `.env` or secret management, and clearly label any fallback values as development-only.

metadata.meta_confidence_reason: The embedded generator in SKILL.md clearly contains literal connection strings and database credentials (`postgres:postgres`). This is real, though low-risk because it is a local development template rather than credential theft or exfiltration.
metadata.meta_exploitability: Easy to reproduce by using the provided template as-is; risk arises from accidental reuse in real deployments.
metadata.meta_impact: Could encourage insecure defaults, accidental exposure of services using trivial credentials, and poor secret-handling practices.

findings.title: Database connection string with embedded credentials

metadata.meta_confidence_reason: The cited line contains an actual hardcoded PostgreSQL connection string with embedded credentials in the Node.js app example.
metadata.meta_exploitability: Easy; anyone generating or copying the template inherits the hardcoded credentials.
metadata.meta_impact: May propagate weak/default credentials into development or misconfigured shared environments.

findings.title: Database connection string with embedded credentials

metadata.meta_confidence_reason: The cited line contains an actual hardcoded PostgreSQL connection string with embedded credentials in the Python app example.
metadata.meta_exploitability: Easy; anyone generating or copying the template inherits the hardcoded credentials.
metadata.meta_impact: May propagate weak/default credentials into development or misconfigured shared environments.

scan_metadata.llm_overall_assessment: The skill appears generally benign and focused on local Docker Compose workflow assistance. No prompt injection, exfiltration, command injection, obfuscation, or availability abuse was identified. The main concerns are low-severity hygiene issues: hardcoded development credentials in the embedded template generator, missing optional manifest metadata, and a referenced file inconsistency.

meta_correlations.group_name: Hardcoded development database credentials in compose generator
meta_correlations.relationship: These findings all refer to the same underlying issue: the embedded Python example in SKILL.md emits Docker Compose configuration with hardcoded PostgreSQL credentials/connection strings for app/api services and the database service.
meta_correlations.consolidated_remediation: Replace literal credentials with placeholders such as `${DATABASE_URL}` and `${POSTGRES_PASSWORD}`, add a `.env.example`, and label any fallback values as development-only and non-production.

meta_correlations.relationship: These findings are related non-security metadata/integrity issues: missing referenced file context, absent allowed-tools declaration, and missing license.
meta_correlations.consolidated_remediation: Add explicit manifest metadata (`license`, optionally `allowed-tools`) and remove or supply any referenced files so the package contents match documentation.

meta_recommendations.title: Parameterize generated database credentials
meta_recommendations.fix: Change the example generator to emit environment-variable placeholders instead of literals, e.g. `DATABASE_URL: ${DATABASE_URL}` and `POSTGRES_PASSWORD: ${POSTGRES_PASSWORD}`, and provide a `.env.example` with non-secret sample values.

meta_recommendations.title: Clearly mark the compose template as development-only
meta_recommendations.fix: Add an inline comment or section header stating `For local development only; do not reuse default credentials in production` and use obviously fake placeholders rather than real-looking secrets.

meta_recommendations.title: Align package metadata with contents
meta_recommendations.fix: Add a proper YAML manifest with `license` and, if desired, `allowed-tools: [Bash, Python]`, and either include `project.py` if intended or remove the reference from packaging metadata.

meta_risk_assessment.summary: This skill appears benign and focused on local Docker Compose workflows; the only substantive issue is insecure hardcoded default database credentials in the example generator.
meta_risk_assessment.top_priority: Replace hardcoded `postgres:postgres` credentials and embedded connection strings with environment-variable placeholders or clearly development-only examples.
meta_risk_assessment.verdict_reasoning: No evidence of prompt injection, exfiltration, command injection, or malicious behavior exists in the provided skill content. Findings are mostly informational manifest/documentation issues plus insecure example defaults.
